The memo: -
Sand mining, storage and sale of sand / sand through online system by the Government.
The policy was issued under the Government Decision dated 19.04.2023. Reforms in the sand policy

The revised sand policy has been issued under the Government Decision dated 16.02.2024. As per these sand policies, sand from the river / basin is to be mined, transported to the depots, created and managed by the depots.
The tender received at the minimum rate in the tender drawn at the level of the Collector is considered.

The quantity of sand sold online through the auction system to the tenderer up to the date 20.12.2025, as per the provisions of the rules and the amount of funds made available by the Department of Finance.
Funds are being paid as follows: -
(Amount Rs.)
table_trans: The name of the sand depot
Payment for sand excavated and sold by date 20.12.2025
Amount to be distributed as per this Government Decision

para_trans: Government Memorandum No: Gaukhani - 10/0424 Q. No.48 / B-1
02. Total amount to be distributed to the Depot in the above plaque is Rs. 1,02,86, 110 / - (Characters)
Rupees One Crore Two Lakh Eighty Six Thousand One Hundred and Ten only) paid subject to the following terms and conditions:
The Government is approving to: -
1) The Collector shall ensure that the amount has not been paid to the tenderer earlier.
2) Sale by the bidder at the time of expenditure of funds sanctioned and received under this order.
The Collector should ensure that not more than 90 per cent of the amount of sand done is paid.
3) Ensure that the sanctioned funds are not spent on works other than those approved by the government.
Take it.
(4) There shall be no funds remaining under the head of principal account mentioned in the said order or
Funds received under the order will be credited to any of the relevant system's Operational Bank Accounts / PLAs.
The concerned should ensure that the deposit is not made in (personal loan account).

5) The expenditure statements / reports in this regard should be submitted to the Government without fail. The above fund-related expense reconciliation statements along with the Reconciliation Slip of the Accountant General's Office
It should be submitted to the government without fail.
6) Utilisation Certificate of funds disbursed under this order to be submitted immediately to the Office of the Accountant General, concerned Collector / Divisional Commissioner / Government.
Do it.
7) Further disbursement of funds without receipt of Utilisation Certificate by the Government.
Note that this will not be done.
8) The Collector who awarded the tender for the said sand depot from the District Mineral Trust Fund to the bidders.
The amount that would have been provided earlier can be transferred to the District Mineral Trust.
The Collector will be responsible.
Expenditure in this regard, demand No.C-01, principal from the grant sanctioned in the financial year 2024-25.
Accounts Head 2853 Non-ferrous Mines and Metallurgical Industries, 02 Regulation and Development of Mines 102 -
Mineral Exploration (00) (01) 06-Industry and Metals 00-Industry and Metals (0) (Datamat) The cost of the tender released for the cost of sand mining, storage, transportation, etc.
to be subtracted from the sanctioned grants available. The computer code of the scheme is No.

The Deputy Secretary (B-1), Revenue and Forest Department is the "Controlling Officer" and the District Mining Officer is the "Drawing and Disbursement" for disbursement of funds from the sanctioned grants available under this head.
The officer "and" head of the office "shall be the" Additional Collector "of the district concerned.
The Government decision is being issued as per the consent given under the Informal Reference No. 275/2024 Expenditure-9 dated 10/07/2024 of the Department of Finance.
The Government decision is available on the Government of Maharashtra website www.maharashtra.gov.in.
